name: pre-publish-review
tagline: Orchestrates up to 16 background sub-agents (ultrabrain, review-work, oracle) to gate an npm release before publish.
verdict: Well-scoped, read-only review workflow with explicit negative triggers and clear output contracts — no security red flags found. Caveats: it depends on custom orchestration primitives (task(), skill(), background_output(), oracle/ultrabrain agent types) specific to the oh-my-openagent framework, undeclared in frontmatter, so it will not run as-is in a plain

highlights.summary: Titled '16-agent' (line 6) but the skill directly spawns only 10 ultrabrains + 1 review-work coordinator + 1 oracle = 12 tasks (Phase 3 table, lines 304-308); the other 4 are nested inside the review-
Handles the 'not yet published / first release' edge case (line 61) but gives no guidance for a background agent that errors, times out, or never returns — Phase 3 only says 'do not deliver until all

Relies throughout on non-standard orchestration primitives — task(), skill(), background_output(), subagent_type='oracle', category='ultrabrain', model='gpt-5.6-sol' (lines 33, 90-94, 167-171, 198-203
